title: [Bug]: PyPI artifact validation import snippet references wrapper-only venv variable
summary: The generated PyPI artifact validation script runs an isolated Python import smoke check with `python -c`, but the snippet references a wrapper variable that is not defined inside that isolated interpreter.
observed: After the validation container installed the public package, the import smoke check failed with a `NameError` because the inline import snippet referenced the validation virtual environment variable from the outer script scope.
expected: The isolated import smoke snippet should embed the expected virtual environment path as data so it can verify the installed module path without relying on wrapper-local variables.
reproduction: Run the local PyPI artifact validation script against the latest public package after the temporary filesystem supports native Python wheels.
failing_test: A focused unit test should inspect the generated validator script and prove that the import smoke snippet embeds the virtual environment path as literal data for the isolated interpreter.
impact: The post-release artifact validation harness cannot complete its public package import check, so maintainers cannot rely on the new issue #252 QA evidence.
delivery_semantics: This bug does not affect NATS delivery or sink ACK behavior. It affects only the local post-release validation harness.
security: The fix must keep source-tree isolation intact and must not mount the repository into the validation import path.
acceptance: The generated import smoke snippet does not reference wrapper-only variables inside the isolated interpreter.
The generated import smoke snippet still verifies that `nats_sinks` imports from the validation virtual environment.
The live PyPI artifact validation container proceeds past the import smoke check.
Focused unit tests cover the generated import snippet behavior.
tests: Add a focused unit test for the generated validator script and rerun the local PyPI artifact validation container.
documentation: No public documentation change is expected beyond issue #252 evidence unless the validation behavior changes for users.
closeout: Close after the generated validator imports the PyPI artifact successfully inside the isolated container process and test evidence is posted.
